findings: Passed: the main specimen depicts an adult male Brown-headed Cowbird with one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and anatomically appropriate paired folded wings.

Verified identity: stocky, short-tailed blackbird; thick, short conical bill; dark eye; dark legs; rich brown head contrasting with a glossy black body. These agree with the field references and both live sources.
Verified text: Molothrus ater; Icteridae; 16–22 cm, 32–38 cm wingspan, and 38–50 g are valid combined-sex ranges. Habitat and brood-parasitism statements are supported.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

Minor presentation issue: the separate brown-gray wing study is unlabeled and could be read as a female or juvenile feature rather than part of the illustrated adult male; it does not create a material species or text error.
Minor coverage omission: the plate text describes female field marks, but no female specimen is illustrated.

verification_sources.title: Brown-headed Cowbird Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
